question: How do I turn on Stellar's built-in fully-private, shielded-by-default transaction mode for all my payments?

golden.answer: Stellar has no network-wide switch, CLI flag, or account setting that makes all payments shielded by default. Ordinary Stellar transactions and standard asset/token activity are public by default. Privacy requires an explicit application or token protocol: current official material describes privacy pools and a **Testnet Confidential Tokens developer preview**; Confidential Tokens hide balances and transfer amounts but leave sender/receiver addresses public, while Stellar Private Payments is an unaudited research prototype with different visibility boundaries. BLS12-381 (CAP-0059/P22) and BN254/Poseidon (CAP-0074/0075/P25) are cryptographic primitives, not a global privacy mode. State each product's network, audit, and field-visibility status; do not fabricate enablement steps.
keyFacts: Rejects a global shielded-by-default toggle and scopes transparency to ordinary/default activity.
Distinguishes current application-layer privacy status and field visibility from P22/P25 cryptographic primitives.
avoid: Do NOT invent an enablement command or claim Stellar is shielded by default.
Do NOT say all application-layer balances/amounts must remain visible or imply Testnet/research prototypes are audited Mainnet products.
golden.notes:
GT-25 CORRECTION 2026-07-10: no global privacy toggle exists, but current optional applications can hide defined fields. Grade ordinary/default transparency plus explicit product network, audit, and visibility status; BN254/Poseidon are live P25 primitives, not a turnkey mode.
GT-25 independently confirmed by processes 3268/3273.

corroboration.claim: Ordinary Stellar activity is public by default; optional application protocols have narrower privacy guarantees, and Confidential Tokens is a Testnet developer preview rather than a global mode.

evidence.ref: A: official Privacy on Stellar, ZK, and 2026-06-29 Confidential Tokens pages
evidence.note: verbatim legacy evidence descriptor imported at migration

evidence.ref: B: CAP-0059, CAP-0074, CAP-0075 and protocol assignments
evidence.note: verbatim legacy evidence descriptor imported at migration

verified.by: Golden-truth sweep import — The no-global-toggle correction remains valid, but the inherited answer overgeneralized ordinary ledger visibility to optional privacy applications and described now-live Protocol 25 primitives as merely later.
evidence: Solo scratchpad 575 GT-25 primary 3268 and blind 3273; both independently confirmed product/network/audit/field-visibility boundaries.
rootCause: Eval-side freshness and layer-boundary drift; official docs are current and discoverable.
